item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 21 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 04h30 on Saturday 21 March 2026.General situation:A light to moderate airstream is flowing over our region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Clouds coming from the Eastern sector will cause few showers mainly over the Eastern part of the island and over the Central plateau early this morning.The weather will be mainly fair during the day.The maximum temperature will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Partly cloudy during the night with occasional showers over the highgrounds.The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at 10 to 20 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 2 metres.High tides:14h31 and tomorrow 02h25.Low tides:08h25 and 20h42.Sunrise:06h13.Sunset:18h20.Atmospheric pressure at 04h00:1013 hectopascals. Weather news for Mauritius issued at 04h30 on Saturday 21 March 2026. General situation:A light to moderate airstream is flowing over our region. Forecast for the next 24 hours:Clouds coming from the Eastern sector will cause few showers mainly over the Eastern part of the island and over the Central plateau early this morning.The weather will be mainly fair during the day.The maximum temperature will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions. Partly cloudy during the night with occasional showers over the highgrounds.The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts. Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at 10 to 20 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 2 metres. Atmospheric pressure at 04h00:1013 hectopascals. item.title: Vol avec violence à Port-Louis : Deux suspects arrêtés
item.description: Un homme a été victime d’un vol avec violence aux environs de Line Barracks, à Port-Louis. Il a été agressé par trois individus. Selon les premiers éléments de l’enquête, les suspects lui ont demandé une cigarette avant de s’en prendre à lui devant son refus. Ils lui ont dérobé son téléphone portable ainsi que Rs 2 000 avant de prendre la fuite. Blessée lors de l’agression, la victime a reçu des soins. Deux suspects ont été interpellés. L’enquête se poursuit. The post Vol avec violence à Port-Lo

item.title: Google Translate exposes Russian spy unit
item.description: Have we just seen the most embarrassing intelligence failure Russia has suffered in years? Investigative group The Insider revealed how a secret Russian unit - known as Center 795 - allegedly ran assassinations and covert operations abroad, but was exposed in a remarkably simple way: one of its agents used Google Translate. According to the investigation, that allowed the FBI to read their communications in real time. So how did a supposedly “untraceable” elite unit unravel so easily, and what d

Trzaskowski argues that a long-standing consensus in Polish politics – being pro-European and pro-Ukraine – has been broken, as populist forces try to outdo each other in an effort to attract voters. item.title: Flights, petrol cars and cruise ships: Amsterdam bans fossil fuel ads
item.description: Cities around the world are clearing their billboards of ads for flights, cruise ships and petrol cars in an effort to reduce planet-warming gases. Amsterdam is the latest city to join the movement, becoming the first capital in the world to approve a legal ban on fossil fuel advertising. Dutch activists are now calling for restrictions to be enforced on a national level. Our Down to Earth team reports.

But few truly expected with such speed, has reshaped the emotional and economic landscape of the region.

item.title: Rose-Hill : De la drogue découverte après un accident de la route
item.description: Un accident de la route survenu à Rose-Hill a conduit à la découverte d’une substance suspectée d’être de la drogue. L’accident, impliquant une voiture et un scooter, a fait deux blessés, le conducteur du deux-roues et son passager. Ils ont été transportés à l’hôpital. Selon les premiers éléments de l’enquête, le conducteur de la voiture présentait des signes laissant penser qu’il était sous l’influence de substances illicites. Une fouille du véhicule a permis la saisie de cinq feuilles d’alumin

item.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
item.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

item.title: Pointe-aux-Sables : Un canot volé sur la plage publique
item.description: Un cas de vol a été rapporté à Pointe-aux-Sables, où une embarcation a été dérobée sur une plage publique. Selon les informations, un canot en fibre de verre a disparu entre le 9 et le 12 mars. La valeur du bien volé est estimée à Rs 125 000. Une enquête a été ouverte pour retrouver l’embarcation et identifier le ou les auteurs de ce vol. The post Pointe-aux-Sables : Un canot volé sur la plage publique appeared first on IONNEWS.

item.title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig
item.description: K-pop megastars BTS released a new album Friday ahead of their open-air comeback concert in Seoul, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people. It will be BTS's first concert after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service.

item.title: 'Harm at a population level': World Happiness Report flags social media's negative impact
item.description: Young people have a “much lower” level of wellbeing than 15 years ago due to the effects of social media use, according to the 2026 edition of the World Happiness Report, which sees Finland maintain its position as the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

item.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
item.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

item.title: Dana Lixenberg's personal, political and pared-down portraits
item.description: Her photographs of celebrated personalities, from Toni Morrison to Tupac Shakur, have become iconic images. Dana Lixenberg tells us about three decades of providing her unique perspective on the United States and the evolution of a society she first started documenting in the 1990s. As her major, monographic exhibition "American Images" opens at the Maison Européenne de la Photo in Paris, the Dutch photographer tells us about being surprised by the enduring success of her beautiful black-and-whi

item.title: Sudanese artists rebuild cultural life in wartime shelter
item.description: In a school-turned-shelter on the Red Sea coast, music rises above the hardships of war as displaced Sudanese artists recreate a sense of community and purpose.

item.title: 🔴 Live: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant statement on Persian New Year as blasts rock Tehran
Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
item.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that even overwhelming force cannot guarantee safety when asymmetric tools, such as drones and decentralised command structures, remain effective. At its core, he contends that reopening the Strait is not a technical or logist

item.title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence, claiming she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
item.description: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

item.title: Tanzania: TMA Issues an Alert Warning of Heavy Rainfall Across the Tanzanian Regions
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a weather alert warning of heavy rainfall across multiple regions, urging residents to take appropriate precautions to avoid potential hazards caused by adverse weather conditions.

item.title: Japan's PM walks tightrope in high-stakes Washington visit
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi travelled to Washington hoping to strengthen ties with the United States. The visit turned into a delicate balancing act amid US President Donald Trump's growing pressure on allies to help the US in the Iran war. Yuka Royer discusses the visit with James Schoff, Senior Director at the Sasakawa Peace Foundation USA.

item.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians caught in the crossfire of Middle East war
item.description: Four women were killed in the occupied #WestBank following missile fire from #Iran - the first Palestinian victims of the Middle East #war since it started on February 28. For Palestinians, it is yet another conflict they find themselves caught up in, with little protection.

item.title: New WTO report: high oil, gas prices serious challenge for trade growth
item.description: The World Trade Organization warned Thursday that prolonged high oil and gas prices in 2026 would "shave off" growth from an already grim trade forecast.

item.title: Nigerian Muslims mark End of Ramadan with Eid al-Fitr prayers
item.description: Muslims in Nigeria and around the world marked the end of the fasting month of Ramadan and ushered in the holiday of Eid al-Fitr on Friday with prayers.

item.title: Namibia: Gondwana - Miss Universe 1992 Returns to Namibia
item.description: [Namibian] More than three decades after Michelle McLean was crowned Miss Universe, she is finally welcoming the friends who shared that moment with her - not on stage, but on a journey across her home country.

item.title: Europe's housing crisis: Is the EU plan up to the job?
item.description: MEPs recently approved a key report on housing, with the stated aim of securing decent, sustainable and affordable housing. They are responding to an EU-wide crisis in which property prices jumped by 15.5 percent between 2021 and 2024, according to Eurostat. That has frozen out millions of people looking to get onto the property ladder. Rental prices have continued to go up as well. Housing is a competence of member states, but there has been a push to do more at the EU level. We debate just how

item.title: French jihadist sentenced to life in jail over IS group genocide of Iraq's Yazidis
item.description: A court in France has sentenced jihadist Sabri Essid for his part in "the genocide perpetrated by the Islamic State group" against Iraq's Yazidi minority, in the first French trial of its kind. Essid is presumed to have been killed in 2018, but without proof of his death he was tried and convicted in absentia.

item.title: Burkina Faso’s tomato export ban raises concerns over regional supply
item.description: Burkina Faso’s decision to ban the export of fresh tomatoes is drawing attention across West Africa, with traders and consumers watching closely for possible effects on supply and prices.

item.title: Nowruz in Tehran: Crowded markets dispte ongoing airstrikes
item.description: Iran’s capital Tehran showed signs of cautious activity on 19 March 2026 as residents prepared for Nowruz, despite ongoing US-Israeli strikes that have disrupted daily life since 28 February.

item.title: Cameroon delays parliamentary elections, lawmakers to stay until end 2026
item.description: Cameroonian lawmakers yet again voted on Thursday to extend their mandate, according to official media.

item.title: Nowruz in London: Iranian diaspora marks spring amid grief over war in Iran
item.description: Members of the Iranian diaspora in north London gathered on 20 March to mark Nowruz, the Persian New Year, despite concern over the ongoing war in Iran.

item.title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
item.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

item.title: Madagascar’s president orders polygraph for aspiring ministers
item.description: Madagascar's military leader, President Michael Randrianirina, announced Thursday that anyone applying to become a minister in the new government will undergo lie detector tests to screen for corruption—a rare and unprecedented vetting measure.

item.description: Egyptians attended prayers at a mosque in Cairo on Friday to mark the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, and bid farewell to the to the Islamic holy month of Ramadan.

item.description: [Vanguard] The 2026 Eid el-Fitr sallah festival was observed peacefully across Maiduguri, the Borno State capital, despite the fear over the renewed bomb explosions and attacks on residents and military formations across the state.

item.description: [Daily Maverick] A group of Durban residents has urged the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court to authorise a class action lawsuit against the Indian agrochemicals giant UPL for health injuries and financial damage they allegedly suffered after a toxic pesticide inferno.

item.title: Gulf crisis draws attention to African oil [Business Africa]
item.description: Unlike crude from the middle east, a lot of African oil does not have to transit vulnerable corridors such as the Strait of Hormuz, where events like war can put a stop to shipping. item.title: UK and Nigeria agree deal to speed up deportations
item.description: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have signed a new agreement aimed at accelerating the return of Nigerians without legal status in Britain, including failed asylum seekers, visa overstayers and convicted offenders.

item.description: A Tunisian court on Thursday sentenced anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah to eight years in prison on financial misconduct charges, a ruling her lawyer condemned as "shocking" and politically motivated, marking the latest crackdown on civil society in the North African country.

item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- THE Tanzania Ending Child Marriage Network has pledged to continue advocating for policy reforms to ensure the legal age of marriage for girls is raised to 18 years.

item.description: French industrialist Vincent Bolloré will stand trial in December before the Paris criminal court in the so‑called “African ports” case.

item.title: Ki zistwar zordi ? [20 mars 1345] La conjonction planétaire et les origines de la peste noire
item.description: Le 20 mars 1345, une conjonction rare entre Mars, Jupiter et Saturne dans le signe du Verseau a captivé l’attention des érudits de l’Université de Paris, alors considérée comme le centre intellectuel de l’Europe. Sollicités par le roi Philippe VI pour expliquer l’émergence d’une maladie foudroyante, les membres de la faculté de médecine ont conclu que cet alignement astral avait provoqué une corruption délétère de l’air par une chaleur et une humidité excessives. item.title: M-PESA to stop sharing full phone numbers with merchants, banks by year-end
item.description: Safaricom, Kenya’s largest telecoms operator, plans to extend data minimisation across its mobile money service M-PESA by late 2026, expanding controls that limit the exposure of customer phone numbers in mobile money transactions.

item.title: South Africa's traditional circumcision claims dozens of young lives
item.description: At least 48 boys and young men died during recent initiation ceremonies in South Africa, among them 22-year-old Lamkelo Mtyho, who had no known health issues when he entered a registered school—but never returned.

item.title: South Africa: Fuel Runs Dry in Nelson Mandela Bay
item.description: [Scrolla] Petroleum companies have started limiting fuel in Nelson Mandela Bay, blaming the Gulf war for putting local supplies at risk. Oil companies are now charging surcharges on normal prices, from 5c per litre for petrol to around R10 per litre for paraffin.

item.title: Kenya: Nyongo Rallies Kisumu Residents Ahead of Ruto-Museveni SGR Launch
item.description: [Capital FM] Kisumu -- Kisumu Governor Prof. Anyang' Nyong'o has called on residents to turn-out in large numbers for the forthcoming visit by President William Ruto to launch the extension of the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Naivasha to Kisumu.

item.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
item.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

item.title: Tributes pour in from across the world for South African peace advocate
item.description: Anti-apartheid activist, human rights campaigner, and constitutional lawyer, Nicholas “Fink” Haysom, died on Wednesday aged 73.

item.title: Tunisia: Tunisian Independence - Between Political Negotiations and Armed Resistance
item.description: [Tunis Afrique Presse] Tunis -- March 20, 1956 was not an ordinary date, but a decisive milestone in Tunisia's contemporary history. On that day, after 75 years of French colonial domination, Tunisia broke with a past of subjugation, following the signing in Paris of the independence protocol.

item.title: Liberia: Boakai, Senators Tight-Lipped On Conference Amid Insecurity At Liberia-Guinea Border
item.description: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- President Joseph Boakai and members of the Liberian Senate are maintaining silence over the outcome of a crucial closed door meeting held at the Capitol Building in Monrovia, as unease continues to grow over mounting tensions along the border between Liberia and Guinea.

item.title: Africa: After Centuries, TB Is Still the Bridesmaid, Never the Bride
item.description: [spotlight] The wins are there but the underlying reality remains, writes Professor Kogie Naidoo as she likens TB to a bridesmaid awaiting its turn to garner attention. The analogy is relevant given the overwhelming number of new TB infections and deaths each year globally for a disease persistently sitting in the shadow of other communicable and non-communicable diseases.

item.title: Morocco/Senegal: CAF Faces Pressure As Guinea Contests Morocco's 1976 Afcon Win
item.description: [Vanguard] The Guinea Football Federation has urged the Confederation of African Football (CAF) to review the 1976 AFCON outcome, citing Morocco's walkout and a recent ruling against Senegal.

item.title: Kenya: Kenya On Alert As Us-Iran-Israel Tensions Heighten Global Risks - CS Tuya
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Defence Cabinet Secretary Soipan Tuya has assured Kenyans that the country remains vigilant in the face of evolving global security threats, including developments linked to US- Iran - Israel conflict and wider Middle East instability.

item.description: [allAfrica] Rwanda is improving its surgical care with the opening of a newly renovated surgical department at Kibungo Level Two Teaching Hospital, previously known as Kibungo Provincial Referral Hospital - a milestone that brings critical services closer to the community.

item.title: Colombia: Torrential rain triggers severe flooding in Facatativá, over 1,000 affected
item.description: Torrential rain that began on 18 March triggered severe flooding in Facatativá, a town 40 km west of Bogotá, affecting more than 1,000 families, according to local authorities.

item.title: Tear gas and tension in Jerusalem as Eid prayers held outside al Aqsa
item.description: For the first time in decades, Al Aqsa is closed during Eid al Fitr, forcing Jerusalem worshippers to pray outside amid security tensions and tear gas.

item.description: Donald Trump joked about Pearl Harbor while urging Sanae Takaichi to expand Japan’s role in securing the Strait of Hormuz, drawing a muted response.

item.description: Rwanda's Ministry of ICT and Innovation launched Innovate Rwanda, a digital platform designed to connect startups, investors, and talent in the country.

item.description: The 2025-26 Budget made provision for a revenue receipt of Rs 10 Billion which was supposed to be forthcoming from the Chagos deal which, however, has not materialized. *“Do not count your chicken before they are hatched”* is an old adage. item.title: Paul Bérenger démissionne de son poste de DPM : «Mo leker kase»
item.description: Dans la prolongation jouée par la presse, nous avons interrogé Paul Bérenger à propos de ses sous-entendus sur le *«gang des cinq»*, l’un des sujets de dissension entre le PM Navin Ramgoolam et lui. L’animal politique a fait dans le sarcasme : *«Ki ou le mo dir ou ? Kan mo koz li koripsion, li dir ou li pena prev. Kom si kan dimounn fer koripsion li pou dir ‘kit enn resi’ !»* Dans *«les jours à venir», comme il l’a maintes fois répété, il reviendra sur ce «dossier» ...

item.title: Répercussions du conflit au Moyen-Orient : Navin Ramgoolam prévient : «Li pou enn bidzet dir, mo bien sagrin»
item.description: Interrogé par la presse après la cérémonie de la communauté télougoue pour Ugadi, jeudi, au Mahatma Gandhi Institute, le chef du gouvernement a, entre autres abordé le contexte international tendu, marqué par le conflit au Moyen-Orient. «Ena bato pe fer letour. Savedir lesans pou monte. Nou pena swa parski nou pa prodir petrol.» Il a également prévenu, fataliste, que ce conflit aura des répercussions sur le coût pour les Mauriciens : «Li pou enn bidzet dir, mo bien sagrin.» ...

item.title: Leptospirose : Curepipe contre-attaque face à l’invasion de rats
item.description: Cité comme l’une des villes les plus touchées par la prolifération de rats, au même titre que Port-Louis, Curepipe a décidé de passer à l’offensive. Face à la recrudescence des cas de leptospirose à travers le pays, la municipalité déploie un plan d’action d’envergure mêlant dératisation massive, opérations de nettoyage et responsabilisation citoyenne. Julien Permal, conseiller municipal, souligne que cette mobilisation s’inscrit dans une stratégie globale visant à r ...

item.title: Alerte sanitaire : Laits infantiles : l’ACIM appelle à la vigilance
item.description: L’association des consommateurs de l’île Maurice (ACIM) a tenu une conférence de presse mercredi afin d’alerter le public sur une situation jugée préoccupante concernant des laits infantiles. L’association a annoncé le rappel de certains lots de laits pour nourrissons des marques Aptamil et Nursie, à la suite d’une alerte sanitaire internationale relayée par les autorités compétentes. Selon les informations disponibles, ces produits pourraient contenir une toxine appelée *«c ...

item.title: «Democracy Report 2026» : Le pays sort de la zone de recul démocratique
item.description: Dans un monde où la démocratie connaît un recul historique, Maurice apparaît comme une exception relative. C’est ce qui ressort du *Democracy Report 2026* publié par V-Dem Institute, une référence mondiale en évaluation de systèmes politiques. Ce constat intervient alors que le pays enregistre également une avancée notable sur le plan de la transparence économique : le Premier ministre, Navin Ramgoolam, avait annoncé mardi à l’Assemblée nationale que Maurice adhère désormais ...

item.title: Rodrigues : Des pépinières de coraux pour restaurer l’avenir marin
item.description: Face aux effets du changement climatique, la restauration des récifs coralliens demeure une priorité à Rodrigues. Dans le cadre de la mission de suivi du portefeuille du Fonds d’adaptation, l’équipe s’est rendue sur l’île pour évaluer les progrès du projet *Restoring Marine Ecosystem Services by Rehabilitating Coral Reefs to Meet a Changing Climate Future*, piloté par le Programme des Nations unies pour le développement en partenariat avec les autorités locales, des organisatio ...

item.title: Paul Bérenger démissionne de son poste de DPM : «Mo leker kase»
item.description: En deux minutes de prise de parole dans son bureau de Deputy Prime Minister à Port-Louis, Paul Bérenger a annoncé la nouvelle : *«Aujourd'hui vendredi je démissionne comme vice-Premier ministre.»* *«Dans la journée d'hier, un ami commun à Navin et à moi m'a fait savoir que Navin Ramgoolam est embarrassé à continuer dans la manière que les choses partent. Tout de suite, naturellement, je l'ai appelé et j'ai demandé s'il souhaite que je me retire comme Premier ministre adjoint, i ...

item.title: Des Mauriciens à Anfield : «Après les Turkish Delight, revanche contre Paris !»
item.description: Xavier-Luc Duval et Ruben Pavaday étaient aux premières loges pour voir Liverpool démonter Galatasaray, 4-0, mercredi à Anfield, en 8es de finale de la Ligue des champions, et manifester leur joie. Les deux Mauriciens ne savaient pas trop à quoi s’attendre avant cette manche retour, dans le sillage de la défaite en Turquie, 1-0, la semaine dernière, et de la contre-performance face aux Spurs dimanche dernier (1-1). *«On avait un peu peur avant ce match»*, avoue Ruben Pavaday, *«Li ...

item.title: Affaire Yogeshwaree Bhunjun : L’interrogatoire du Dr Arvind Ramchurn renvoyé
item.description: Nouvau rebondissement dans l’enquête sur la disparition jugée inquiétante de Yogeshwaree Bhunjun. L’interrogatoire du Dr Arvind Ramchurn, considéré comme un maillon clé dans cette affaire, n’a finalement pas eu lieu comme prévu. Selon son homme de loi, Mᵉ Rama Valayden, cette audition a été renvoyée à une date ultérieure en raison de l’état de santé du médecin, retardant ainsi une étape jugée cruciale par les enquêteurs. item.title: Parc automobile : La nouvelle BMW iX3 100 % électrique lancée à Maurice
item.description: La transition locale vers la mobilité électrique a une nouvelle incarnation automobile. Le concessionnaire Leal Group a officiellement lancé, le 10 mars, la nouvelle BMW iX3, un *«Sport Activity Vehicle»* (SAV) entièrement électrique, qui incarne la nouvelle génération technologique du constructeur allemand. item.title: Interview : Ram Etwareea : «Je ne serais pas surpris que le leader annonce son départ ce vendredi»
item.description: 🔵 **Quelle est votre réaction sur la situation où encore une fois depuis les élec- tions, Paul Bérenger, leader du MMM, semble incertain quant à son avenir au gouvernement et quel est l’état d’esprit des autres membres du parti ?** On ne va pas se le cacher : le Mouvement militant mauricien (MMM) traverse une nouvelle crise ou un nouveau chapitre dans la même crise. item.title: Durcissement des conditions d’entrée : Visa US : jusqu’à 15 000 dollars de caution pour les Mauriciens
item.description: Le département d’État américain a annoncé l’extension de son programme de caution pour les visas, qui s’appliquera désormais à un total de 50 pays à compter du 2 avril. Dans ce cadre, les ressortissants de ces pays devront verser une caution comprise entre 5 000 et 15 000 dollars avant d’obtenir un visa de type B1 ou B2, destiné aux voyages d’affaires ou de tourisme aux États-Unis. Cette caution sera remboursée aux bénéficiaires qui respectent les conditions de leur visa ...

item.title: 30ᵉ Championnat des clubs de la zone 7 : Volley-ball : Des motifs de satisfaction malgré les obstacles rencontrés
item.description: Malgré des conditions loin d’être idéales, les équipes mauriciennes ont su faire preuve de caractère lors du 30ᵉ Championnat des clubs de la zone 7 disputé aux Seychelles. Les dames du Quatre-Bornes VBC ont signé la meilleure performance en décrochant une médaille de bronze après une montée en puissance au fil du tournoi, tandis que les hommes du QBVC et le Port-Louis Fire Star ont eux aussi tiré des enseignements précieux d’une compétition relevée sur la scène régionale. ...

item.title: En Inde : Le petit cœur d’Ezekiel progresse en attendant sa chirurgie
item.description: Ezekiel Aaron Cleo, âgé de quatre mois, continue de montrer des signes positifs après son transfert en Inde pour une chirurgie cardiaque vitale. Selon les dernières informations communiquées par le Dr Naveen Chaudhri, de l’équipe Overseas Medical Care Assistance (OMCA) India, le nourrisson est actuellement stable et tous ses paramètres vitaux sont normaux. item.title: Politique : Probable démission de Bérenger comme DPM ce vendredi
item.description: L’incertitude plane autour de l’avenir politique de Paul Bérenger. Le Deputy Prime Minister a convoqué la presse ce vendredi 20 mars à 11 heures, dans son bureau à Port-Louis, pour une annonce décisive. Cette prise de parole intervient dans un contexte tendu au sein du Mouvement militant mauricien (MMM). item.description: *«Pena Konsey de minis vandredi, pena parski ti ena vizit deta prezidan, pena Cabinet Papers e Kabine sa vandredi-la.»* Lors de sa conférence de presse tenue le mercredi 18 mars, à l’issue du Comité central du MMM, Paul Bérenger, Deputy Prime Minister (DPM), a affirmé qu’il n’y aurait ni Conseil des ministres ni *Cabinet Papers* ce vendredi. item.title: Conflit au Moyen-Orient : Les perturbations aériennes font chuter les réservations des locations de vacances
item.description: L’association des activités de location saisonnières à l’île Maurice (AALSIM), qui regroupe depuis 2018 les opérateurs du secteur de la location de vacances – représentant environ 11 000 emplois et plus de Rs 8 milliards de recettes annuelles – tire la sonnette d’alarme. item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Zimbabwe's Catholic bishops have raised concerns over the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3, saying it may have far-reaching implications on the country's democratic processes.

item.title: Collège St Mary’s West : 25 ans d’éducation inclusive et tournée vers l’humain
item.description: Le Collège St Mary’s West a célébré ses 25 ans d’existence lors d’une cérémonie tenue le 17 mars, marquée par un appel à une éducation plus inclusive et centrée sur le développement humain. Présent à l’événement, l’évêque de Port-Louis, Mgr Jean Michaël Durhône, a salué une institution qu’il qualifie de « prophétique », rappelant que dès sa création, le collège a fait le choix d’accueillir des élèves aux profils et capacités variés, en rupture avec une vision élitiste de l’éducation catholique.

item.description: La ministre des Services financiers et de la planification économique, Jyoti Jeetun, a appelé à une transformation du secteur de l’assurance face à un environnement économique mondial de plus en plus incertain. Diesel a partagé la nouvelle sur Instagram, publiant une photo en noir et blanc de lui-même en réunion avec Lesslie pour discuter du scénario de ce final de franchise tant attendu, dont la sortie en salles est prévue pour le 17 mars 2028.
